prs.title: Add LaTeX report for retail market analysis
prs.body:
📄 Retail Market Behavior – Project Report

The Retail Market Behavior Analysis Report presents a comprehensive study of customer purchasing patterns using a multi-task data mining pipeline. The report documents the full analytical process—from data preprocessing to modeling and insights—based on the UCI Online Retail dataset containing over 500,000 transaction records.

This technical document details the methodology and results for:

RFM-based Customer Segmentation using K-Means clustering to identify high-, medium-, and low-value customer groups

Product Similarity Search using K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and text-embedded product descriptions

Market Basket Analysis through the Apriori algorithm to uncover frequently co-purchased items

Temporal Sales Analysis using hourly and weekday revenue aggregation

Interactive Dashboard Integration showcasing all analytical modules in a unified interface

The report highlights key business insights such as customer value distribution, meaningful item-to-item relationships, peak purchasing hours, and strong co-occurrence product sets. It also outlines limitations, validation strategies, and recommendations for future enhancement of the analytics pipeline.

This document serves as the formal written deliverable for the project, summarizing both the theoretical foundations and the practical implementation of the system.

prs.title: docs: update outdated Svelte Society editor support link URL
prs.body:
**Fixes** https://github.com/sveltejs/kit/issues/14941

This PR addresses the issue of the support link in the Editor Tooling section of the Getting Started documentation resulting in a "No content found" page.

---

### What's Fixed

Updated the link within the 'documentation/docs/01-introduction/02-getting-started.md' file. The text and surrounding formatting have been preserved exactly as they were, with **only the URL being modified**.

* **Old URL:** `https://sveltesociety.dev/resources#editor-support`
* **New URL:** `https://sveltesociety.dev/collection/editor-support-c85c080efc292a34`

### Why This Change Matters

The old link resulted in a "No content found" error, causing confusion for new users seeking editor setup instructions. This change ensures the documentation provides **accurate, working resources** so beginners can easily find supported editor integrations.

---

### Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following

- [ ] It's really useful if your PR references an issue where it is discussed ahead of time. In many cases, features are absent for a reason. For large changes, please create an RFC: https://github.com/sveltejs/rfcs
- [x] Prefix your PR title with `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, or `docs:`.
- [x] This message body should clearly illustrate what problems it solves.
- [ ] Ideally, include a test that fails without this PR but passes with it.
- [ ] If this PR changes code within `packages/svelte/src`, add a changeset (`npx changeset`).

### Tests and linting

This PR only updates documentation and does not modify any source code or logic.

- [x] Run the tests with `pnpm test` and lint the project with `pnpm lint` (N/A, documentation change only)

No changeset is needed.

prs.title: docs: update editor setup section
prs.body:
Fixes #14941

This PR updates the “Editor setup” section in the SvelteKit documentation to remove an outdated Svelte Society link that no longer provides editor support information. The previous text implied broad editor support via that page, but the page has been empty for some time.

### What this PR fixes
- Removes the broken Svelte Society editor-support link.
- Rewrites the sentence to avoid implying official support for “numerous other editors”.
- Clarifies that while community extensions exist for various editors, support varies and may not fully cover SvelteKit features.

This avoids confusing users and reflects the current state of editor tooling more accurately.

This is a documentation-only change; no package version bump is required, so no changeset is needed.


---

### Please don't delete this checklist! Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:
- [x] It's really useful if your PR references an issue where it is discussed ahead of time. In many cases, features are absent for a reason. For large changes, please create an RFC: https://github.com/sveltejs/rfcs
- [x] This message body should clearly illustrate what problems it solves.
- [ ] Ideally, include a test that fails without this PR but passes with it.
  - _Not applicable — documentation-only change._

### Tests
- [x] Run the tests with `pnpm test` and lint the project with `pnpm lint` and `pnpm check`
  - _All checks pass locally._

### Changesets
- [x] If your PR makes a change that should be noted in one or more packages' changelogs, generate a changeset by running `pnpm changeset` and following the prompts.

### Edits
- [x] Please ensure that 'Allow edits from maintainers' is checked. PRs without this option may be closed.

prs.title: docs: add TypeScript example for custom axios instance with typed interceptors
prs.body:
**Summary**
This PR addresses **Issue #7261** by adding a comprehensive TypeScript example to the README that demonstrates how to create a custom axios instance with fully typed request and response interceptors.

**Problem**
TypeScript users frequently ask how to properly type axios interceptors when creating custom instances. The existing TypeScript section in the README only shows basic error handling with `axios.isAxiosError()`, but doesn't demonstrate:
- How to type request interceptors with `InternalAxiosRequestConfig`
- How to type response interceptors with `AxiosResponse`
- How to handle errors with typed `AxiosError` in interceptors
- How to make typed API calls using generics
This created a documentation gap that led to confusion and repetitive questions from the community.


**Solution**
Added a new subsection **"Using Axios with TypeScript — Custom Instance + Typed Interceptors"** under the existing TypeScript section that includes a complete, practical example demonstrating:
1.Importing the necessary TypeScript types from axios
2.Defining a typed interface for API responses (`User`)
3. Creating a custom axios instance with configuration
4. Adding a typed request interceptor using `InternalAxiosRequestConfig`
5. Adding a typed response interceptor using `AxiosResponse`
6.Handling errors with `AxiosError` in interceptors
7. Making typed API calls using generics (`apiClient.get<User>()`)
8. Proper error handling in async functions
## Benefits
- **Improved Developer Experience**: TypeScript users can immediately see how to properly type their axios instances and interceptors
- **Reduced Confusion**: Clear, copy-paste-ready example following axios conventions
- **Best Practices**: Demonstrates idiomatic TypeScript usage with axios
- **Practical Use Case**: Shows a real-world scenario (auth token injection, global error handling)

**Modified Files:**
- `README.md` - Added new TypeScript subsection with comprehensive example

**Related Issue**
Closes: #7261
[Documentation] Add TypeScript example for custom instance with interceptors

**Status**
This PR is complete and ready for review.

prs.title: fix(schema): correctly merge folder-based multi-file Prisma schemas in Prisma 7
prs.body:
This PR fixes an issue in Prisma v7.x where .prisma model files inside nested folders (e.g., prisma/models/*.prisma) were not being merged into the final schema when using the documented prismaSchemaFolder configuration. Only models defined directly in schema.prisma were recognized, leading to missing models in the generated client, incomplete migrations, and inconsistent schema state.

This behavior worked in Prisma 6.12.x but regressed in Prisma 7 due to changes in the schema loading pipeline.

What Was Broken

Given a structure like:

prisma/
  schema.prisma        // generator + datasource only
  models/
    user.prisma
    payment.prisma


and config:

export default defineConfig({
  schema: "prisma/schema.prisma",
  prismaSchemaFolder: "prisma",
});


Prisma CLI failed to:

Load .prisma files from nested directories

Merge models/enums from sibling files

Include them in Prisma Client

Include them in migrations

Display them in Prisma Studio

Root Cause

The schema engine resolved only the entry schema path but did not recursively collect .prisma files under the prismaSchemaFolder.
As a result, the internal AST contained only the content of schema.prisma, ignoring all subfile definitions.

Fix Overview

✔ Implemented recursive traversal of all .prisma files within the folder defined by prismaSchemaFolder
✔ Ensured deterministic merge ordering and stable output
✔ Ensured all models/enums feed into:

Code generation

Migrations

Prisma Studio

Validation
✔ Added integration tests covering folder-based schemas
✔ Verified no regressions for single-file schemas

Test Plan

Local monorepo setup:

pnpm -r run dev
cd sandbox
cp -r basic-sqlite multi-file-test


Folder structure:

prisma/
  schema.prisma
  models/
    user.prisma
    payment.prisma


Commands tested:

pnpm prisma format
pnpm prisma generate
pnpm prisma migrate dev --name init
pnpm start


Verified:

 All models were detected

 Prisma Client was generated with all models

 Migrations included all tables

 Prisma Studio displayed all models

 No breaking changes observed

Breaking Changes

None.
The behavior now matches Prisma’s documented expectations for multi-file schemas.

Fixes #28678


Additional Notes

This fix restores full multi-file schema support for Prisma 7, enabling large and structured schema layouts without workarounds.
It also aligns Prisma 7’s behavior with the previously stable setup in Prisma 6.12.x.

prs.title: Fix/vite internal paths matching route
prs.body:
fix(router): exclude Vite HMR internal paths from `{-$locale}` route

When accessing the dev server over a network IP, paths like `/@react-refresh`
and `/_vite/...` were incorrectly being parsed as locale codes in the
`/{-$locale}/` route. This caused Vite HMR requests to be routed through the
application, breaking HMR and returning full HTML responses.

A beforeLoad check now rejects locale values starting with '@' or '_', as these
are never valid locale codes.

Also removed unused proxy logic in `entry-server.tsx`, cleaned up debug logs,
and fixed a minor comment typo.
